item.title: Following the Money: The 82-Wallet Bitcoin Cluster Linked to Iran’s IRGC-CEC
item.description: Explore FalconFeeds’ investigation into an 82-wallet Bitcoin cluster linked to IRGC-CEC’s Shahid Hemmat unit, tracing funding flows, laundering architecture, exchange exposure, and live sanctions implications.

item.title: Cyber Islamic Resistance-Axis and the Iran-Israel Shadow War
item.description: Explore Cyber Islamic Resistance-Axis, a pro-Iranian hacktivist group operating within the Axis of Resistance, and its role in the Iran–Israel shadow war through DDoS attacks, infrastructure targeting, and ideological cyber operations.

item.title: Iran's FindFace Acquisition: The Architecture of a Digital Surveillance State
item.description: Iran’s acquisition of Russia’s FindFace facial recognition platform marks a major step toward a Chinese-style surveillance state, combining biometric identification, CCTV integration, and digital authoritarian control.

item.title: WIPED IN 79 COUNTRIES: The Handala Hack Attack on Stryker Corporation
item.description: A detailed threat intelligence blog on the Handala Hack attack against Stryker Corporation, examining the destructive cyberattack that wiped systems across 79 countries and its broader Iran-linked implications.

item.title: Cyber Refuge Zones: Where Threat Actors Operate Safely and Why It Matters
item.description: Explore how Cyber Refuge Zones give threat actors safe operating environments through weak laws, political protection, non-extradition barriers, and illicit financial infrastructure and why these digital safe havens matter for global cybersecurity.

item.title: Cyber Escalation Signals: CTI Indicators That Predict Conflict-Driven Attack Waves
item.description: Explore the CTI indicators that signal conflict-driven cyber escalation, from threat actor pre-positioning to geopolitical triggers, and how predictive intelligence helps defend critical infrastructure before attack waves begin.

item.title: Critical Infrastructure as a Message: Why Threat Actors Target Energy and Telecom During Tensions
item.description: Explore why threat actors target energy, telecom, and other critical infrastructure during geopolitical tensions, and how these cyber operations serve as strategic signals of capability, coercion, and intent.

item.title: The Conquerors Electronic Army and MASAV: How a Hacktivist Group Quietly Targeted the Backbone of Israeli Interbank Payments
item.description: A pro-Palestinian hacktivist group, Conquerors Electronic Army (CEA), targets Israel’s MASAV payment system with DDoS and cyber operations, highlighting the growing role of financial infrastructure disruption in conflict-driven cyber warfare.

item.title: From Shadows to Sanctions: Unmasking 35 Iranian Cyber Operatives Driving State-Sponsored Warfare
item.description: An intelligence-driven analysis exposing 35 Iranian cyber operatives, front companies, and state-linked networks tied to IRGC and MOIS operations, based on DOJ indictments, OFAC sanctions, FBI advisories, and private-sector threat intelligence.

item.title: Operation Phantom Claude: A macOS Infostealer Campaign Exploiting Anthropic’s Brand Identity
item.description: A sophisticated macOS infostealer campaign dubbed “Operation Phantom Claude” is exploiting trust in Anthropic’s Claude AI, using fake branding, Cloudflare infrastructure, and multi-stage loaders to deliver credential-stealing malware. FalconFeeds intelligence reveals a coordinated attack targeting macOS users with advanced evasion techniques and AI-themed social engineering.

item.title: MicroStealer / SparkStealer: Large-Scale Gaming-Themed Credential Theft Infrastructure Uncovered
item.description: FalconFeeds reveals a large-scale MicroStealer and SparkStealer campaign abusing Minecraft-themed domains to target gamers via fake mods and server links, enabling credential theft, wallet exfiltration, and account compromise.

item.title: UNC1069: DPRK’s Deepfake-Driven Cyber Campaign Targeting Crypto and Software Supply Chains
item.description: UNC1069, a DPRK-linked threat actor tied to the Reconnaissance General Bureau, is leveraging deepfake-driven social engineering and supply chain attacks—including npm compromise—to target crypto and developer ecosystems, marking a new evolution in North Korean cyber operations.

item.title: Dark Storm Team: Hacktivism at Scale in the Iran–Israel Cyber Conflict and Middle East Critical Infrastructure Threat Landscape
item.description: An in-depth FalconFeeds analysis of Dark Storm Team’s role in the Iran–Israel cyber conflict, exposing its threat to Middle East governments, critical infrastructure, and Abraham Accords-aligned states.

item.title: Commercial Satellite Intelligence as a Geopolitical Weapon: Satellite Navigation, GPS Warfare & the Iran - U.S. Conflict
item.description: An in-depth analysis of how satellite navigation, GPS jamming, spoofing, and China’s BeiDou system became strategic weapons in the Iran–U.S. conflict, disrupting shipping, oil flows, and regional power dynamics.

item.title: Why Falcon Feeds Stands Out as the Ultimate One-Stop CTI Feed Solution for Modern Security Operations Centers
item.description: Discover why Falcon Feeds is the ultimate one-stop CTI solution for SOC teams with real-time threat intelligence, faster detection, and simplified security operations.

item.title: Iran - Israel Conflict Malware Landscape: Common Malware Families, Technical Analysis, IOC Compendium & Attribution Mapping
item.description: Explore the evolving malware ecosystem of the Iran–Israel conflict with a technical breakdown of key families, IOCs, MITRE ATT&CK mappings, and threat actor activity using data from FalconFeeds.io, AlienVault OTX, and VirusTotal.

item.title: Commercial Satellite Internet in Modern Conflict: The Strategic Role of Starlink & Commercial SATCOM in Contemporary Warfare, Intelligence Operations & Geopolitical Power Realignment
item.description: Explore how Starlink is reshaping modern conflict through battlefield connectivity, drone operations, and geopolitical power shifts across Ukraine and Iran, highlighting the risks of private control over critical wartime infrastructure.

item.title: Manufacturing Under Siege: Ransomware Convergence on Industrial Targets
item.description: Explore the 20 April 2026 ransomware surge where INC RANSOM, CoinbaseCartel, CHAOS, and The Gentlemen targeted global manufacturing, exposing risks to industrial and supply-chain organizations.

item.title: HexagonalRodent: DPRK AI-Powered Crypto Theft Campaign Using LinkedIn Lures to Target Web3 Develop
item.description: Explore how HexagonalRodent used AI-powered LinkedIn phishing and malware to steal $12M from Web3 developers, revealing new tactics in DPRK crypto theft operations.

item.title: Vidar & StrelaStealer IOC Alert: Fresh Infrastructure Signals Active Credential Theft Campaigns
item.description: Discover new IOCs linked to Vidar and StrelaStealer, revealing active infrastructure, phishing distribution, and immediate risks to organizations worldwide.

item.title: Structural Resilience and the Hydrated Adversary: A Multi-Dimensional Analysis of Threat Actor Migration and Operational Displacement
item.description: Explore how cyber threat actors adapt to global crackdowns through migration, infrastructure resilience, and AI-driven operations, highlighting the evolving dynamics of modern cybercrime ecosystems and operational displacement.

item.title: Iran-Linked Cyber Escalation: Mapping Retaliatory Threat Actor Campaigns Post-Conflict
item.description: Explore how Iran escalated cyber operations after Operation Epic Fury, mapping APT activity, hacktivist campaigns, and attacks on critical infrastructure.

item.title: Critical Infrastructure at Risk: The Strategic Convergence of Cyber-Physical Threats in the 2026 Landscape
item.description: Explore rising cyber-physical threats to Critical National Infrastructure as APTs, ransomware groups, and hacktivists target IT/OT environments in 2026.

item.title: Inside the LiveDNS Phishing Campaign: How Typosquatted Domains Are Targeting Israeli Users
item.description: Learn how attackers impersonating LiveDNS Ltd are using typosquatted domains and fake renewal emails to steal payment information from Israeli domain owners.

item.title: Brazil 2026: Mapping Pre-Election Disinformation Infrastructure and Narrative Seeding
item.description: Explore how narrative seeding and disinformation infrastructure are emerging ahead of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election through coordinated activity across X, TikTok, and Google Trends.

item.title: Beyond DDoS: How NoName057(16) Became a Tool of Geopolitical Pressure
item.description: Explore how NoName057(16) evolved beyond DDoS attacks into a geopolitical pressure instrument, synchronizing cyber disruption with political events across Europe and NATO-aligned states.

item.title: UAT-8302: China-Linked APT Deploying Shared Malware Arsenal Against Governments Across Three Continents
item.description: Explore how UAT-8302 leverages shared malware ecosystems, custom implants, and stealth persistence techniques to target government entities across multiple continents.

item.title: GitHub Under Siege: TeamPCP, LAPSUS$ & DPRK-Linked Abuse Converge on the Developer Ecosystem
item.description: Explore the 2026 GitHub breach linked to TeamPCP and LAPSUS$, alongside DPRK-linked GitHub abuse campaigns targeting developers and software ecosystems.

item.title: KEYMOUS+: Inside the North African Hacktivist-DDoS Empire Driving Global Cyber Disruption
item.description: Explore how KEYMOUS+ became one of the world’s most active hacktivist DDoS actors through global disruption campaigns, alliance networks, and DDoS-for-hire infrastructure.

item.title: THE GENTLEMEN: Inside the Russia-Linked Ransomware Operation Rapidly Reshaping the RaaS Ecosystem
item.description: Explore how The Gentlemen evolved into one of 2026’s fastest-growing RaaS programs through aggressive affiliate incentives, FortiGate exploitation, and leaked internal infrastructure.

item.title: Shai-Hulud: From npm Worm to Cross-Ecosystem Supply Chain Threat
item.description: Explore the Shai-Hulud campaign that evolved from an npm worm into a cross-ecosystem threat compromising npm and PyPI through CI/CD abuse and stolen developer credentials.

item.title: SideWinder: The India-Linked APT Expanding from South Asia to Global Strategic Infrastructure
item.description: Explore the evolution of SideWinder from a South Asian espionage actor to a global cyber threat targeting governments, maritime infrastructure, and nuclear facilities.

item.title: Semiconductor Sanctions & the Cyber Espionage Nexus: How Export Controls Are Reshaping Global Cyber Conflict
item.description: Explore how semiconductor export controls, sanctions, and cyber espionage are converging to reshape global technological competition, targeting chip manufacturers, supply chains, and critical intellectual property.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Mapping the Global Cyber Scam Ecosystem Targeting Fans
item.description: Explore the cyber scam landscape surrounding the FIFA World Cup 2026, including phishing domains, ticket fraud, crypto scams, fake streaming platforms, and social engineering campaigns targeting fans worldwide.

item.title: From Breach to Influence: The Convergence of Cyber Espionage, Ransomware, and Strategic Narrative Warfare
item.description: Explore how cyber espionage, ransomware, and influence operations are converging through hack-and-leak campaigns, turning stolen data into a powerful tool for narrative warfare and geopolitical pressure.

item.title: Threat Actors Without Borders: The Rise of Transnational Cybercrime Alliances
item.description: Explore how transnational cybercrime alliances exploit jurisdictional gaps, global infrastructure, and cyber-enabled fraud to operate across borders and challenge traditional law enforcement frameworks.

item.title: The New Reconnaissance Era: How Threat Actors Profile Organizations Before the First Phish
item.description: Explore how threat actors use OSINT, AI-powered reconnaissance, and breach intelligence to profile organizations and employees before launching targeted phishing and cyber intrusion campaigns.

item.title: The Next Billion Devices: How Connected Infrastructure Is Reshaping Threat Intelligence
item.description: Explore how billions of IoT devices, connected infrastructure, and edge computing are reshaping threat intelligence, expanding attack surfaces, and creating new cybersecurity challenges worldwide.

item.title: The Middle East Cyber Arms Race: Who Is Gaining Strategic Advantage?
item.description: Explore the Middle East cyber arms race and how geopolitics, state-sponsored cyber operations, and critical infrastructure threats are reshaping the region's strategic security landscape.

item.title: The Telegram Underground: Mapping Threat Actor Ecosystems in 2026
item.description: Explore how Telegram continues to power global cybercrime in 2026, enabling threat actor coordination, underground marketplaces, malware distribution, and resilient criminal ecosystems despite increased enforcement.

item.title: No Threat Actor Works Alone: Understanding the Cybercrime Supply Chain
item.description: Explore the cybercrime supply chain and how ransomware groups, initial access brokers, malware developers, and shadow agents collaborate to power today's global threat ecosystem.

item.title: The First 72 Hours: An Intelligence-Led Analysis of Post-Compromise Behavior and Attacker Velocity
item.description: Explore how threat actors exploit the first 72 hours after compromise through rapid lateral movement, IAB partnerships, and accelerated ransomware operations that outpace traditional SOC defenses.

item.title: FortiBleed: How Millions of Fortinet Credentials Became a Cybercriminal Goldmine
item.description: Explore the FortiBleed credential exposure campaign, analyzing compromised Fortinet credentials, large-scale password cracking, affected organizations, and enterprise security risks worldwide.

item.title: Threat Actors in 2026: The Emerging Groups Every CISO Should Watch
item.description: Explore the emerging cyber threat actors of 2026, including state-sponsored APTs, ransomware groups, and AI-enabled adversaries targeting cloud, identity, and critical infrastructure.

item.title: The Rise of Cyber Mercenaries: How Private Threat Actors Are Reshaping Global Conflict
item.description: Explore how cyber mercenaries and commercial surveillance vendors are reshaping global conflict by selling advanced offensive cyber capabilities to governments worldwide.

item.title: Threat Actors vs. Critical Infrastructure: The Strategic Shift from Data Theft to Operational Disruption
item.description: Explore how cyber threat actors are shifting from data theft to operational disruption, targeting critical infrastructure through AI-driven attacks, OT compromise, and geopolitical cyber campaigns.

item.title: Threat Actors Are Training AI Too: What CTI Teams Need to Know
item.description: Explore how threat actors are using AI, LLMs, and machine learning to automate cyberattacks and what cyber threat intelligence teams need to know to stay ahead.

item.title: Inside DieNet: From DDoS Collective to Multi-Vector Cyber Threat Group
item.description: Explore DieNet's rise from a DDoS collective to a multi-vector cyber threat group, its LockNet ransomware operations, attack evolution, and eventual operational shutdown.

item.title: Election Security in Latin America: Mapping Cyber Threats to Democratic Institutions
item.description: Explore the cyber threats facing elections and democratic institutions in Latin America, from ransomware and disinformation to election infrastructure attacks and cybersecurity preparedness.

item.title: The Cyber Domino Effect: How One Regional Incident Reshapes Global Threat Activity
item.description: Explore the cyber domino effect and how regional cyber incidents cascade through supply chains, cloud services, and critical infrastructure to create global cybersecurity risks.
